response.step_by_step_log: 1. Analyzed the video content. 2. Identified the intro skit (00:00-02:18) and the subsequent song performance (02:18-05:00). 3. Used on-screen credits to identify the song as 'ばかみたい' (Baka Mitai) and its composer, 福山光晴 (Mitsuharu Fukuyama). 4. Searched for lyrics using 'ばかみたい 福山光晴 歌詞'. 5. Verified lyrics and translated them for the English reference section. 6. Structured data into the final JSON format.
response.global_summary: The video begins with a lighthearted skit featuring two characters, Croy and Gibriella, followed by a musical performance of the song 'Baka Mitai', which they sing together on stage.

scenes.description: The video starts with a conversation and skit between two animated characters, Croy and Gibriella, sitting in a room. This ends with a title card reading 'DROPPED OUT OF SCHOOL MUSICAL' and related Japanese text.
Croy and Gibriella stand on a stage and perform the song 'Baka Mitai'. On-screen text credits the song title ('ばかみたい'), lyrics writer ('堀井亮佑'), and composer ('福山光晴').

scenes.reference_lyrics_en:
Baka Mitai (Like a fool)
I'm just a child, aren't I?
Chasing dreams and getting hurt
Even though you're bad at lying
You showed me a smile you couldn't laugh with
You wouldn't even say 'I love you' properly
You're bad with words and truly clumsy
Yet, yet, why
Could you say goodbye?
It's no good, it's bad, it's just no good
I love you so much, I love you too much
No matter how strong the alcohol is
The memories won't get distorted
Like a fool
Like a fool, you're really a fool
Always believing in you
Pretending to be a strong woman
Bathed in the night wind of heartache
Three years have passed since I was left alone
Even the streets have changed
Only the attachment is left behind
You're really not a good man
I'll take off our matching rings
